transcript: Mullen High School, may I help you?
Hi, do you have a... um, gym teacher?
Do you have a gym teacher?
Well, yeah, we have all kinds of gym teachers.
No, I'm sorry. They are all gone for the day.
Can I enroll my s... my son there?
Well... I don't know about this year, but, uh... you can do that for next year.
Mm, I need to get him in right away.
Well, I'll tell you what. Brother Marvin would be in charge, because-- '[beep]' --he's in charge of admissions...
Uh, no, I don't have any brothers. Did--
I don't have any brothers, neither does my son, he's an only child. I just need to enroll him, please, if that'd be feasible.
Sir, what I'm saying is, Brother Marvin is in charge of admissions.
Uh huh. I don't... I don't have any... admissions to make to you. I admit I would like my son to go there. Um, when... what kind of s... lunches do you serve for the kids?
Mmhmm. Okay, so what time should I have him there for first-- [beep] --hour tomorrow?
Oh, sir... um, sir, you can't bring him in. You have to have him enrolled and registered.
Oh, he is. Oh, he can do all that tomorrow during lunch, or somethin'. Ah, when... would... does the class start?
I'm sorry, sir, but you need to speak with Brother Marvin first.
Um... does he give ointment to the kids?
Yes, he would... uh huh... but you have to speak with him first.
Oh, he does deal with ointment, then?
I will have to have him give you a call.
Could I have your name and number, and I'll have him call you.
Do the... well, let me ask... okay, can I ask you one more question?
Um... did you say you have cold lunches? Do the kids get ointment sandwiches?
No, I don't believe so, I never heard of that.
